dialogues: You proved something important. No matter which array you check, the two expressions always give the same product. This shows us that you can multiply the factors in any order and always get the same answer. These are turn-around facts.
Mathematicians have a name for this. They call it the commutative property of multiplication. But the idea is simple. You can change the order of the factors and get the same answer.
And here's why this is more than just interesting. It's a strategy, a plan for solving problems. If you know one fact, you already know its turn-around fact without doing any extra work.
